title: mentha arvensis var. glabrata
intro: Mentha arvensis var. glabrata, commonly known as American wild mint, is a variety of the mint plant that is native to North America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic agent. The health benefits of American wild mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
